free_text: Measurement of 4He charge- and mass-changing cross
sections on H, C, O, and Si targets in the energy
range 70-220 MeV/u for radiation transport
calculations in ion-beam therapy
Institute of Medical Physics and Radiation
Protection, THM University of Applied Sciences, Giessen
Heidelberg Ion-Beam Therapy Center, Heidelberg
National Centre for Oncological Hadrontherapy
Pavia
a 5-mm-thick plastic scintillator
BC-400-like and a 14-cm-thick BaF2 scintillator.
The measured cross sections are given for
the energy at the center of the thickest target used
with an uncertainty interval covering the energy loss
before and after the target center.
VS
The uncertainty estimation considers the
statistical component originating from the finite
amount of measured events as well as systematic
components introduced by the application of fit
methods and extrapolations into overlap regions in
the measured spectra.
Table 1 of Phys.Rev.C99(2019)014603

CH2 samples with density of 0.947 g/cm3 and thicknesses

able to check for robustness of the data analysis
method.

Derived by subtraction of the elemental C cross section
from those measured on CH2 compound target.
Uncertainties calculated from the
uncertainties of the CH2 and C cross sections assuming
they are not correlated.
